description: Cycle B of prod-readiness plan (2026-05-25 audit) — closes 4 P0/P1 observability gaps. Adds 9 missing metrics (US-001 prereq), 18 SLO-anchored alert rules + 8 SLO recording rules (US-002) + 12 burn-rate alerts (US-003), pprof endpoint behind admin auth (US-004), /operate/slo.md + strata admin slo-report subcommand single-binary-preserved (US-005), 5 new Grafana dashboards workers/clusters/tenants/meta-backends/data-backends (US-006-US-009), dashboard drift-lint Go test extension (US-010), smoke + ROADMAP close (US-011). 11 stories. Pre-launch opt-in defaults. Source PRD at tasks/prd-prod-observability.md (deleted in US-011 closing commit per PRD lifecycle). Critical PRD-review fixes baked in: B1 US-001 metric gap-fill so US-002+ reference only real metrics; B2 durability SLO oracled via always-on strata_gc_terminal_ack_total (not inventory-dependent); B3 pprof decode via github.com/google/pprof/profile.Parse (NOT golang.org/x/perf/pprof which doesn't exist); B4 strata admin slo-report subcommand preserves single-binary invariant (NOT bash+curl+jq script); S1 8 SLO recording rules centralize numerics so SLO target change = 1-file edit; S6 Grafana schemaVersion 39 pinned + drift-lint asserts.

userStories.title: Metric instrumentation gap-fill (9 missing metrics)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the 9 metrics referenced by upcoming alerts and dashboards instrumented so US-002..US-011 can reference only real metric names without ad-hoc inline additions.
acceptanceCriteria: **`strata_heartbeat_last_write_timestamp`** (gauge, no labels): set on every `internal/heartbeat/heartbeat.go::Heartbeater.tick` to `float64(time.Now().Unix())`. Powers upcoming StrataHeartbeatStale alert (US-002).

**`strata_rados_cluster_object_count`** (gauge, labels `cluster`): set on every RADOS health probe per cluster from existing `internal/data/rados/health.go::DataHealth` walk; mirrors existing `strata_bucket_bytes` gauge pattern.
**`strata_rados_cluster_bytes_used`** (gauge, labels `cluster`): same write site as above, sourced from `ClusterStatsProbe.GetPoolStats` aggregate.

**`strata_bucket_quota_bytes`** (gauge, labels `bucket`): updated by existing `internal/bucketstats/sampler.go` tick; reads `meta.Store.GetBucketQuota` per (bucket, sample) and emits `q.MaxBytes` (or 0 if unlimited).

**`strata_tikv_pessimistic_txn_total`** (counter, labels `op, outcome` where outcome∈{commit, rollback, conflict}): bumped inside every pessimistic-txn helper in `internal/meta/tikv/` (verify call sites via `grep -rn 'pessimistic' internal/meta/tikv/`); one bump per outcome path.

**`strata_data_s3_api_calls_total`** (counter, labels `cluster, operation, outcome` where outcome∈{success, error, throttled}): bumped per AWS SDK call inside `internal/data/s3/observer.go` via SDK middleware (alongside existing OTel instrumentation from US-003c of ralph/harden-gateway).
**`strata_data_s3_throttled_total`** (counter, labels `cluster, operation`): bumped when SDK retry chain observes `ThrottlingException` / `SlowDown` / `RequestLimitExceeded` AWS error codes.
**`strata_inventory_objects_total`** (gauge, labels `bucket, configID`): set on every inventory worker tick to the number of objects walked. Sourced from `internal/inventory/`.

**`strata_worker_leader_events_total`** (counter, labels `worker, event` where event∈{acquired, released}): bumped from existing `cmd/strata/workers.Supervisor.LeaderEvents()` channel-publisher site (currently emits to heartbeat only — augment to also bump counter).

Verify `strata_rebalance_migratable_chunks_total` / `_stuck_single_policy_chunks_total` / `_stuck_no_policy_chunks_total` exist via grep on internal/metrics/; if any missing, fold instrumentation into this story (referenced by US-007 cluster dashboard drain-progress panel).

All new histograms (if any added later) MUST use explicit bucket spec — for sub-second ops use `prometheus.ExponentialBuckets(0.001, 2, 16)` (1ms..32s); for byte counts use `prometheus.ExponentialBuckets(1024, 4, 10)` (1 KiB..1 GiB). Add this rule to `internal/metrics/metrics.go` package comment for future use; this story adds no histograms.
Each new metric registered in `internal/metrics/metrics.go::init` registry list (the `prometheus.MustRegister(...)` block).
Unit test `internal/metrics/metrics_test.go` extended: new metrics appear in the `requiredMetrics` set + `Help` strings non-empty.

Live-probe smoke: `make run-memory` + `curl http://localhost:8080/metrics | grep -cE '^strata_(heartbeat_last_write_timestamp|rados_cluster_object_count|rados_cluster_bytes_used|bucket_quota_bytes|tikv_pessimistic_txn_total|data_s3_api_calls_total|data_s3_throttled_total|inventory_objects_total|worker_leader_events_total)'` returns 9.

ROADMAP entry CREATED in `ROADMAP.md` under `## Correctness & consistency` section: `- **P0/P1 — Cycle B: prod-observability (metric gap-fill + alert rules + burn-rate alerts + pprof endpoint + SLO/SLI doc + 5 new Grafana dashboards + dashboard drift-lint).** In progress on ralph/prod-observability. Closes 4 observability gaps from 2026-05-25 audit. Flipped Done on cycle close (US-011).`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped via commit 3c9d8bd. 9 PRD metrics + 3 rebalance categorisation gauges registered + prewarmed; observer adapters wired across heartbeat / rados / bucketstats / tikv / data-s3 / inventory / workers / rebalance; smoke probe confirms 9 sample + 9 HELP lines.

userStories.title: Prometheus alert rules + SLO recording rules
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want shipped alert rules covering every metric in the operator-facing shortlist so I can wire pagers without writing PromQL myself. SLO target values are recording rules so changes ripple cleanly.
acceptanceCriteria: New file `deploy/prometheus/alerts.yml` with rule groups: `strata.recording`, `strata.availability`, `strata.latency`, `strata.workers`, `strata.replication`, `strata.backends`, `strata.security`, `strata.drain`.

**Recording rules** (`strata.recording` group) — 8 rules centralize SLO targets + per-window SLI ratios. Required entries: `strata:slo_availability:target` (expr: 0.999), `strata:slo_latency_get_put_seconds:target` (0.5), `strata:slo_latency_list_seconds:target` (2), `strata:slo_latency_multipart_complete_seconds:target` (1), `strata:slo_durability_error_rate:target` (0), `strata:availability:ratio_rate5m` (expr `1 - (sum(rate(strata_http_requests_total{code=~"5..",bucket!="_admin"}[5m])) / sum(rate(strata_http_requests_total{bucket!="_admin"}[5m])))`), `strata:latency_get_put:p99_rate5m` (expr `histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le) (rate(strata_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket{method=~"GET|PUT"}[5m])))`), `strata:durability:error_rate5m` (expr `sum(rate(strata_gc_terminal_ack_total{reason!="enoent",reason!="ok"}[5m]))`).
Each alert rule carries labels: `severity={critical|warning|info}`, `team=storage`, `slo={availability|latency|durability|<empty>}`.
Each rule carries `annotations.summary` (one-line) + `annotations.description` (template variables) + `annotations.runbook_url` pointing at `/operate/alerts.md#<rule-id>`.

18 alert rules: `Strata5xxRateHigh` (`(1 - strata:availability:ratio_rate5m) > (1 - strata:slo_availability:target) * 14.4` for 5min); `StrataLatencyP99Above500ms` (`strata:latency_get_put:p99_rate5m > strata:slo_latency_get_put_seconds:target` for 5min); `StrataWorkerPanic` (`rate(strata_worker_panic_total[5m]) > 0` for 1min — labels carry worker + shard); `StrataReplicationQueueAge` (`max(strata_replication_queue_age_seconds) > 600` for 10min per-bucket); `StrataReplicationQueueGrowth` (`deriv(strata_replication_queue_depth[15m]) > 0` for 15min); `StrataCassandraLwtConflictSpike` (`rate(strata_cassandra_lwt_conflicts_total[5m]) > 10` for 5min per-bucket-shard); `StrataGCQueueGrowth` (`deriv(strata_gc_queue_depth[15m]) > 0` for 15min); `StrataLifecycleErrorSpike` (`rate(strata_lifecycle_tick_total{status="error"}[5m]) > 0.1` for 5min); `StrataNotifyDLQGrowth` (`rate(strata_notify_delivery_total{status="dlq"}[10m]) > 0` for 10min); `StrataCassandraQueryP99Spike` (`histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, table, op) (rate(strata_cassandra_query_duration_seconds_bucket[5m]))) > 0.1` for 5min); `StrataRADOSOpP99Spike` (`histogram_quantile(0.99, sum by (le, pool, op) (rate(strata_rados_op_duration_seconds_bucket[5m]))) > 0.5` for 5min); `StrataOTelRingbufHorizonLow` (`strata_otel_ringbuf_oldest_age_seconds < 300` for 5min); `StrataAuditStreamSubscriberLeak` (`strata_audit_stream_subscribers > 50` for 10min); `StrataBackendTLSSkipVerify` (`sum(strata_backend_tls_skip_verify) > 0` for 1min — Cycle A gauge); `StrataBucketStatsShardImbalance` (`max by (bucket) (strata_bucket_stats_shard_writes_total) / sum by (bucket) (strata_bucket_stats_shard_writes_total) > 0.5` for 30min); `StrataDrainProgressStalled` (`changes(strata_rebalance_chunks_moved_total[15m]) == 0 and on() strata_drain_complete_total == 0` for 15min during evacuate); `StrataHeartbeatStale` (`time() - max(strata_heartbeat_last_write_timestamp) > 60` for 2min — uses US-001 metric); `StrataRateLimitRefusalSpike` (`rate(strata_ingress_rate_limit_refused_total[5m]) > 10` for 5min — Cycle A counter).
`promtool check rules deploy/prometheus/alerts.yml` exits 0.
`deploy/prometheus/prometheus.yml` extended with `rule_files: [alerts.yml]` so the lab loads them automatically.
New `make promtool-check` Makefile target wraps `promtool check rules deploy/prometheus/alerts.yml` (degrades to exit 0 + WARN when promtool missing, mirrors `make helm-lint` pattern).
CI `.github/workflows/ci.yml` `lint-build` job extended to `go install github.com/prometheus/prometheus/cmd/promtool@latest` + run `make promtool-check`.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped deploy/prometheus/alerts.yml — 8 recording rules in strata.recording group (5 SLO targets + 3 5m SLI ratios) + 18 alert rules across 7 groups (availability, latency, workers, replication, backends, security, drain). All exprs reference real metric names. prometheus.yml wired with rule_files: [alerts.yml]. New make promtool-check target degrades to WARN when promtool missing (mirrors helm-lint pattern). CI lint-build job installs promtool + runs make promtool-check. Local promtool installed via prometheus-3.6.0.darwin-arm64 tarball; promtool check rules reports SUCCESS: 26 rules found.

userStories.title: Burn-rate alerts (4-window multi-burn-rate) + /operate/alerts.md
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want 4-window multi-burn-rate alerts (Google SRE workbook ch.5) for the top 3 SLOs so I can distinguish slow-burn from fast-burn budget consumption.
acceptanceCriteria: `deploy/prometheus/alerts.yml` gains a `strata.burn_rate` rule group with 12 rules (4 windows × 3 SLOs).

**All burn-rate expressions reference recording rules from US-002** — no hard-coded SLO numerics. Example shape: `(1 - strata:availability:ratio_rate5m) > (1 - strata:slo_availability:target) * 14.4 and (1 - strata:availability:ratio_rate1h) > (1 - strata:slo_availability:target) * 14.4` with `for: 2m` + labels `{severity: critical, team: storage, slo: availability}`.

Recording rules from US-002 extended with matching per-window SLI ratios for burn-rate: add `strata:availability:ratio_rate1h`, `strata:availability:ratio_rate6h`, `strata:availability:ratio_rate1d`, plus `strata:latency_get_put:p99_rate1h/_rate6h/_rate1d` and `strata:durability:error_rate1h/_rate6h/_rate1d`.
**Availability SLO** 4 alerts: 5m+1h (14.4× — page critical, `for: 2m`), 30m+6h (6× — page critical, `for: 15m`), 6h+1d (3× — ticket warning, `for: 1h`), 24h+3d (1× — ticket info, `for: 3h`).
**Latency SLO** 4 alerts: same 4-window structure using `strata:latency_get_put:p99_rate*` recording rules.
**Durability SLO** 4 alerts: same structure using `strata:durability:error_rate*` recording rules; per-bucket alert labels carry `bucket` for routing where applicable.
Each alert has `annotations.error_budget_remaining_pct` template using PromQL `(1 - (consumed/budget))`.
`promtool check rules` exits 0 (total: 8 SLO recording rules from US-002 + 9 burn-rate window recording rules + 18 SLO alerts + 12 burn-rate alerts = 47 rules).

New doc page `docs/site/content/operate/alerts.md` created here (NOT US-011 — page is owned by this story). Body: label conventions section (severity/team/slo) + routing guidance (`wire these labels into your Alertmanager routing tree`) + per-rule one-paragraph runbook stub for all 30 alert rules + `Burn-rate alert philosophy` section with table mapping window pair → severity → expected operator action.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped deploy/prometheus/alerts.yml extension — 9 new per-window SLI recording rules (3 SLOs × {1h, 6h, 1d}) folded into strata.recording group + 12 burn-rate alerts in new strata.burn_rate group (4 windows × 3 SLOs per Google SRE workbook ch.5 MWMBR pattern: 5m+1h@14.4× critical, 30m+6h@6× critical, 6h+1d@3× warning, 1d+3d@1× info). 30m + 3d windows inlined (kept off recording rules per PRD AC's 8+9 budget). Each burn-rate alert carries burn_window + burn_rate labels for routing + error_budget_remaining_pct PromQL recipe annotation. Durability SLO target=0 zero-tolerance semantics preserved (multiplier × 0 = 0 → multi-window framing suppresses single-blip noise, severity escalates by window pair). New doc page docs/site/content/operate/alerts.md ships label conventions + Alertmanager routing recipe + burn-rate philosophy table + per-rule runbook stubs for all 30 alerts. Fixed 2 typo'd runbook_url anchors from US-002 (stratradosopp99spike → strataradosopp99spike, stratarratelimitrefusalspike → strataratelimitrefusalspike). promtool check rules → SUCCESS: 47 rules found (matches PRD AC). hugo --minify clean. make vet clean.

userStories.title: pprof endpoint behind admin auth + /operate/profiling.md
userStories.description: As an SRE responding to a perf incident, I want a pprof endpoint protected by admin auth so I can capture heap/CPU/goroutine profiles on demand without exposing them to S3 clients.
acceptanceCriteria: `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go` registers `/debug/pprof/*` handlers (heap, cpu, goroutine, block, mutex, profile, trace, allocs) via explicit `pprof.Index` / `pprof.Cmdline` / `pprof.Profile` / `pprof.Symbol` / `pprof.Trace` + `pprof.Handler("heap")` etc. wires. NOT side-effect import of `net/http/pprof` — Strata does not use DefaultServeMux.
`STRATA_PPROF_ENABLED` env (bool, default `false` — opt-in even when admin listener set; defense-in-depth, pprof exposes heap which may contain sensitive data in error paths).

`STRATA_PPROF_LISTEN` env (default empty = use admin listener if set, else fail-fast at boot if pprof enabled but neither admin nor pprof listener configured). When set (e.g. `127.0.0.1:9002`), pprof gets its OWN listener (third listener after main + admin).
pprof handlers protected by existing `auth.MultiStore`-backed admin auth chain on any listener; shared middleware instance.

Block + mutex profiling REQUIRE explicit `runtime.SetBlockProfileRate(N)` / `runtime.SetMutexProfileFraction(N)` calls — gated by `STRATA_PPROF_BLOCK_RATE` (default 0 = disabled) + `STRATA_PPROF_MUTEX_RATE` (default 0 = disabled) envs.
All 4 envs wired through `Config.Pprof.*` substruct + `envMap` + TOML example `[pprof]` section + reference page (per ralph/toml-parity contract).

**`github.com/google/pprof/profile` added to `go.mod` direct require** (BSD-3-Clause, Google-owned, vetted). NOT `golang.org/x/perf/pprof` which doesn't exist. Verify via `go mod why`; if currently transitive only, promote to direct require.

Unit test `internal/serverapp/pprof_test.go`: (a) disabled = `/debug/pprof/heap` returns 404 on every listener, (b) enabled on admin listener = returns valid pprof bytes (decoded via `github.com/google/pprof/profile.Parse`), (c) enabled on dedicated listener = returns pprof bytes; admin listener 404, (d) admin auth required on dedicated pprof listener.

New helper `internal/serverapp/pprof_decode_test.go`: Go-native pprof decode helper compiled once; smoke script reuses via `go test -run TestPprofDecode` — NO runtime `go tool pprof` dependency, NO BusyBox-fragile shell tooling.

New doc page `docs/site/content/operate/profiling.md`: per-profile coverage (heap=leak diag, cpu=hot-path, goroutine=leak/deadlock, block=lock contention, mutex=mutex contention, trace=scheduling), `go tool pprof` recipes for operator use, flamegraph workflow via `go tool pprof -http :7070 heap.pprof`. Recommends operator dev mode `STRATA_PPROF_SKIP_TOOL_CHECK=1` for environments without `go` binary.
Smoke script `scripts/smoke-pprof.sh` boots Strata with pprof enabled, curls `/debug/pprof/heap`, decodes via Go-native helper.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped opt-in /debug/pprof/* endpoint behind admin auth chain. 4 envs (STRATA_PPROF_ENABLED/_LISTEN/_BLOCK_RATE/_MUTEX_RATE) + [pprof] TOML section wired through Config + envMap + env-vars.md + strata.toml.example (drift-lint green). Routes wired explicitly via pprof.Index/Cmdline/Profile/Symbol/Trace + pprof.Handler('heap'|'allocs'|'goroutine'|'block'|'mutex'|'threadcreate') — no side-effect import of net/http/pprof (Strata never uses DefaultServeMux). Three attach modes: dedicated listener (STRATA_PPROF_LISTEN), admin listener fallback, or fail-fast at boot when both empty. adminapi.Server exposes WrapWithAdminAuth(http.Handler) so pprof reuses the same session-cookie/SigV4 chain that protects /admin/v1/*. github.com/google/pprof promoted to direct require via new internal/pprofutil/decode.go wrapping profile.Parse. Tests: internal/serverapp/pprof_test.go covers disabled-404, admin-listener-serves, dedicated-listener-routing, anon-401, config-validation; pprof_decode_test.go ships TestPprofDecode (smoke entry; reads STRATA_PPROF_SMOKE_PROFILE) + TestPprofDecodeRoundTrip (in-process heap dump round-trip). scripts/smoke-pprof.sh boots strata, captures heap + cpu profiles via scripts/pprof-fetch (SigV4-signed Go one-shot mirroring the test helper), decodes via TestPprofDecode — no go tool pprof needed. docs/site/content/operate/profiling.md ships profile-type matrix + go tool pprof recipes + flamegraph workflow + security caveats + STRATA_PPROF_SKIP_TOOL_CHECK reservation. operate/_index.md card grid extended. Smoke + go vet + go test ./... clean.

userStories.title: SLO/SLI doc /operate/slo.md + strata admin slo-report subcommand
userStories.description: As leadership/SRE/customer, I want a published SLO baseline so I can align tenant SLAs and ship weekly compliance reports. As a maintainer, I want the report tool to live inside the single `strata` binary per the single-binary invariant.
acceptanceCriteria: New doc page `docs/site/content/operate/slo.md` defines: Availability SLO (99.9% non-5xx over 30-day window — SLI formula references `strata:availability:ratio_rate5m` recording rule from US-002); Latency SLO (p99 GET/PUT <500ms, LIST <2s, multipart Complete <1s — formulas reference matching recording rules); Worker durability SLO (error rate of non-ENOENT-non-OK GC terminal acks zero over 90-day window — oracled via `strata:durability:error_rate5m` recording rule using always-on `strata_gc_terminal_ack_total{reason!="enoent",reason!="ok"}`, NO inventory dependency).

Doc explicitly notes: SLO targets are starting points — operators tune per tenant SLA via the recording rules in `deploy/prometheus/alerts.yml`; cross-links to /operate/alerts.md burn-rate section + /operate/monitoring.md metric definitions.
Document recommended Prometheus retention setting (`--storage.tsdb.retention.time=90d` minimum) for 90-day worker durability SLO long-window aggregation.
**NEW `strata admin slo-report` subcommand** at `cmd/strata/admin/slo_report.go` (per project CLAUDE.md `Single-binary invariant` — NO new top-level binary, NO bash+curl+jq script).

Subcommand flags: `--prometheus-url <url>` (default `http://localhost:9090`), `--window <7d|30d|90d>` (default `7d`), `--out <file.md>` (default stdout), `--format <markdown|json>` (default `markdown`), `--alertmanager-url <url>` (optional — if provided, queries burn-rate alert firings count over window).

Subcommand uses `github.com/prometheus/client_golang/api/prometheus/v1` HTTP client (already a transitive dep via Strata's Prometheus client export — verify via `go mod why`; promote to direct require if needed) to query: (a) recording rules from US-002 over window, (b) `topk(5, sum by (path) (rate(strata_http_requests_total{code=~"5.."}[<window>])))`, (c) `topk(5, sum by (path) (rate(strata_http_request_duration_seconds_count{path!=""}[<window>])))` joined with bucket histogram for slow paths, (d) burn-rate alert firings count if --alertmanager-url provided.
Emits Markdown table: per-SLO row (target, actual, status emoji ✅/⚠️/🔥), top-5 5xx paths, top-5 slow paths, burn-rate firings count.
New `make slo-report` Makefile target wraps `bin/strata admin slo-report` with default flags.
Subcommand integration test `cmd/strata/admin/slo_report_test.go`: mocks Prometheus HTTP API with `httptest.NewServer`, asserts Markdown output contains expected SLO rows + path topk tables.
Smoke: `make up-all && make slo-report` produces non-empty markdown referencing all 3 SLOs.
`docs/site/content/operate/_index.md` card grid extended with new entry pointing at `/operate/slo.md`.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Shipped docs/site/content/operate/slo.md — availability/latency/durability baselines with SLI formulas + recording rule references + Prom 90d retention guidance + cross-links to /operate/alerts (burn-rate philosophy + recording rules) + /operate/monitoring + /operate/profiling. strata admin slo-report subcommand (cmd/strata/admin/slo_report.go) preserves single-binary invariant; flags --prometheus-url --window 7d|30d|90d --out --format markdown|json --alertmanager-url; uses internal/promclient (existing net/http wrapper — no new direct require on client_golang/api). Alertmanager v2 GET /api/v2/alerts filtered by slo label; documents currently-firing semantic since AM lacks historical counts. statusForRatioSLO/LatencySLO/DurabilitySLO classify ok/warning/breached with within-10% warning band. slo_report_test.go covers healthy/breached/empty-vector/AM-integration/CLI-markdown/CLI-json/invalid-window paths via httptest Prom + AM mocks. make slo-report Makefile target wraps subcommand with override-friendly SLO_REPORT_FLAGS knob. _index.md card grid extended. env-vars.md adds STRATA_ALERTMANAGER_URL row (CLI-only, em-dash TOML key); STRATA_PROMETHEUS_URL already wired via PrometheusConfig. STRATA_ALERTMANAGER_URL exempted in internal/config/exempt_env_vars.go::ExemptEnvVars.Exact. hugo --minify clean. go vet + go test ./cmd/strata/admin/... + ./internal/config/... clean.

userStories.title: Per-worker Grafana dashboard
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want a per-worker Grafana dashboard so I can drill into a specific worker's iteration count, error rate, panic rate, lease churn, per-tick duration, and queue depth.
acceptanceCriteria: New dashboard `deploy/grafana/dashboards/strata-workers.json` with template variable `worker` ∈ {gc, lifecycle, replicator, notify, access-log, inventory, audit-export, manifest-rewriter, quota-reconcile, usage-rollup, rebalance, reshard}.
**`schemaVersion: 39`** (Grafana 10 baseline) — pin explicit value so drift-lint (US-010) catches accidental downgrade.

Per-tab panels: (a) iteration count, (b) error rate, (c) panic rate (`rate(strata_worker_panic_total{worker="$worker"}[5m])`), (d) leader-lease churn (`rate(strata_worker_leader_events_total{worker="$worker"}[5m])` — uses US-001 metric), (e) per-tick duration histogram (heatmap), (f) queue depth where applicable (gc: `strata_gc_queue_depth`, replicator: `strata_replication_queue_depth`, notify: `strata_notify_queue_depth` if exists else skip).

Iteration counter source: `worker.<name>.tick_total` from OTel iteration span helper (existing) OR new Prom counter `strata_worker_iteration_total{worker}` (fold into US-001 instrumentation if no existing Prom counter — verify at impl time; document decision in commit message).
Dashboard `uid: strata-workers`. Tags `["strata", "workers"]`. Time range default 1h.
Dashboard provisioned via existing `deploy/grafana/dashboard.yaml` provider (auto-loaded by lab Grafana — provider already walks /var/lib/grafana/dashboards/).

**Hero board `strata-dashboard.json` gains a drill-down link via Grafana panel `links` array**: URL pattern `/d/strata-workers?var-worker=$worker` — uses Grafana variable substitution so click on a worker name in hero opens this board with the variable pre-filled.
All PromQL exprs reference real metric names (cross-checked in US-010 drift-lint).
Template variable `worker` uses `refresh: "On Time Range Change"` (NOT `"On Dashboard Load"`) — bounded query cost.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Per-cluster Grafana dashboard
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want a per-cluster Grafana dashboard so I can see chunks/bytes used per RADOS or S3 cluster, drain progress, rebalance moves, refusals, TLS skip-verify gauge.
acceptanceCriteria: New dashboard `deploy/grafana/dashboards/strata-clusters.json` with template variable `cluster` ∈ (all configured cluster IDs from `strata_rebalance_chunks_moved_total` label set).
**`schemaVersion: 39`** pinned.

Per-tab panels: (a) chunks_used (`strata_rados_cluster_object_count{cluster="$cluster"}` — from US-001), (b) bytes_used (`strata_rados_cluster_bytes_used{cluster="$cluster"}` — from US-001), (c) drain progress (3-line plot: migratable / stuck_single_policy / stuck_no_policy from `strata_rebalance_*_chunks_total{cluster="$cluster"}` — verified/folded into US-001 if missing), (d) rebalance moves in/out (`rate(strata_rebalance_chunks_moved_total{from="$cluster"}[5m])` vs `{to="$cluster"}`), (e) refusals (`rate(strata_rebalance_refused_total{target="$cluster"}[5m])` + `rate(strata_putchunks_refused_total{cluster="$cluster"}[5m])`), (f) TLS skip-verify gauge (`strata_backend_tls_skip_verify{cluster="$cluster"}` — Cycle A gauge).
Dashboard `uid: strata-clusters`. Hero drill-down link via `links` array URL pattern `/d/strata-clusters?var-cluster=$cluster`.
Template variable `cluster` uses `refresh: "On Time Range Change"`.
All PromQL exprs reference real metric names (cross-checked in US-010 drift-lint).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Per-tenant Grafana dashboard
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want a per-tenant (access_key + bucket) Grafana dashboard so I can see request rate, error rate, bytes throughput, quota utilization, rate-limit refusals per customer.
acceptanceCriteria: New dashboard `deploy/grafana/dashboards/strata-tenants.json` with template variables `access_key` + `bucket` (multi-select).
**`schemaVersion: 39`** pinned.

Per-row panels: (a) request rate (`sum by (method) (rate(strata_http_requests_total{access_key="$access_key",bucket="$bucket"}[5m]))` — verified labels access_key + bucket exist on strata_http_requests_total), (b) error rate by code class, (c) bytes uploaded/downloaded (gauge from `strata_bucket_bytes{bucket="$bucket"}` + per-request size histogram), (d) quota utilization (`strata_bucket_bytes{bucket="$bucket"} / strata_bucket_quota_bytes{bucket="$bucket"}` — both from existing + US-001), (e) rate-limit refusals (`rate(strata_ingress_rate_limit_refused_total{reason=~"key|ip"}[5m])` — Cycle A counter).
Dashboard `uid: strata-tenants`. Hero drill-down link via `links` array URL pattern `/d/strata-tenants?var-access_key=$access_key&var-bucket=$bucket`.

**Cardinality bound**: template variables use `query_result()` with `topk(50, ...)` to avoid blowing up picker on 10k+ buckets. `refresh: "On Time Range Change"` so re-query cost only on time-range change.
All PromQL exprs reference real metric names (cross-checked in US-010 drift-lint).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Per-meta-backend + per-data-backend Grafana dashboards
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want per-meta-backend + per-data-backend Grafana dashboards so I can isolate Cassandra-vs-TiKV health and RADOS-vs-S3-upstream throughput.
acceptanceCriteria: New dashboard `deploy/grafana/dashboards/strata-meta-backends.json`: Cassandra row (LWT conflicts heatmap `sum by (bucket, shard) (rate(strata_cassandra_lwt_conflicts_total[5m]))`, query duration p50/p99 per table `histogram_quantile(0.5|0.99, sum by (le, table) (rate(strata_cassandra_query_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])))`); TiKV row (pessimistic-txn churn per op `rate(strata_tikv_pessimistic_txn_total[5m])` — from US-001, sweeper rate `rate(strata_meta_tikv_audit_sweep_deleted_total[5m])` — existing); Memory row (lab only): row count via `strata_meta_memory_rows_total` if instrumented; else blank panel + note.

New dashboard `deploy/grafana/dashboards/strata-data-backends.json`: RADOS row (op latency p50/p99 per pool `histogram_quantile(0.5|0.99, sum by (le, pool) (rate(strata_rados_op_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])))`, put/get/del throughput); S3-upstream row (API call rate per cluster `rate(strata_data_s3_api_calls_total{outcome="success"}[5m])` — from US-001, throttling `rate(strata_data_s3_throttled_total[5m])` — from US-001, error rate `rate(strata_data_s3_api_calls_total{outcome="error"}[5m])`); Memory row (lab only): RSS proxy from `process_resident_memory_bytes` (Prom built-in).
**`schemaVersion: 39`** pinned on both.
Both dashboards `uid: strata-meta-backends` / `strata-data-backends`. Hero drill-down links via `links` array.
All PromQL exprs reference real metric names (cross-checked in US-010 drift-lint).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Dashboard drift-lint Go test extension
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the existing dashboard drift-lint test extended to cover all 6 dashboards (1 hero + 5 new) so future refactors don't silently break dashboards.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend existing `deploy/grafana/dashboard_test.go` to walk every `*.json` file under `deploy/grafana/` AND `deploy/grafana/dashboards/`.
Per dashboard: parse JSON → walk every `panel.targets[].expr` (including nested panels via `panel.panels[]`) → extract metric names via regex `strata_[a-z_][a-z0-9_]*`.

Cross-check each extracted metric against the set of metric names exported by `internal/metrics/` (walk via `go/parser` + `go/ast` on `internal/metrics/metrics.go` looking for `Name: "<metric>"` literals inside `prometheus.<Counter|Histogram|Gauge>Opts{}` or `prometheus.New<...>Vec` calls).
Hard-fail with dashboard path + panel ID + missing metric name.
Assert each dashboard has `schemaVersion >= 39` (Grafana 10 baseline pinned in US-006..US-009).
Exempt list for non-Strata metrics: `process_*`, `go_*`, `prometheus_*`, `node_*`.

Worker iteration counters (`worker.<name>.tick_total` family from US-006 — OTel-instrumented spans, not Prom metrics) explicitly exempted with comment block; OR fold into US-001 instrumentation as Prom counter `strata_worker_iteration_total{worker}` if simpler — document decision in commit message.
Test runs under `go test ./deploy/grafana/...` (no new build tag).
Test currently passes (all dashboards from US-006..US-009 reference real metrics post-US-001).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Smoke + docs polish + ROADMAP flip Done
userStories.description: As an operator, I want end-to-end smoke validation that all features compose cleanly, plus the cycle ROADMAP entry flipped Done.
acceptanceCriteria: New script `scripts/smoke-observability.sh`: (a) `promtool check rules deploy/prometheus/alerts.yml` exits 0 (skip with WARN when promtool missing), (b) starts Strata with `STRATA_PPROF_ENABLED=true STRATA_PPROF_LISTEN=127.0.0.1:19002 STRATA_ADMIN_LISTEN=127.0.0.1:19001`, (c) curls `/debug/pprof/heap` from loopback with admin auth → asserts response decodes via Go-native helper from US-004, (d) `go test ./deploy/grafana/...` passes (dashboard drift-lint green), (e) `bin/strata admin slo-report --window 7d --prometheus-url http://localhost:9090 --out /tmp/slo-report.md` produces non-empty output against the bare lab (Prometheus running via `make up-all`).
New `make smoke-observability` Makefile target wraps the smoke script.

Hero board `deploy/grafana/strata-dashboard.json` updated with 4 top-of-dashboard drill-down `links` entries pointing at new dashboards (one per US-006..US-009 dashboard UID — final integration polish; per-story added panel-level links, this finalizes top-of-dashboard links).

`docs/site/content/operate/_index.md` card grid extended with entries for `slo.md` (US-005), `alerts.md` (US-003), `profiling.md` (US-004) — each created in its owning story; this just adds card grid entries.
`docs/site/content/reference/env-vars.md` extended with rows for `STRATA_PPROF_*` (4 new entries: enabled, listen, block_rate, mutex_rate) + TOML-key column populated.
`deploy/strata.toml.example` extended with `[pprof]` section (4 keys + one-line comment per knob).
Drift-lint test `internal/config/env_toml_parity_test.go` green (new pprof envs accounted for).

**ROADMAP entry FLIPPED to Done** in `ROADMAP.md`: `~~**P0/P1 — Cycle B: prod-observability (metric gap-fill + alert rules + burn-rate alerts + pprof endpoint + SLO/SLI doc + 5 new Grafana dashboards + dashboard drift-lint).**~~ — **Done.** Shipped via ralph/prod-observability cycle (US-001..US-011). 9 new metrics from gap-fill. 18 SLO-anchored alerts + 12 burn-rate alerts + 8 SLO recording rules. pprof opt-in via STRATA_PPROF_*. SLO/SLI doc + strata admin slo-report subcommand (single-binary invariant preserved). 5 new dashboards (workers/clusters/tenants/meta-backends/data-backends) all pinned schemaVersion 39. Drift-lint test extended to all 6 dashboards. (commit <SHA>)`
ALL existing smokes still pass: `make smoke`, `make smoke-tikv-default-lab`, every `scripts/smoke-*.sh` shipped.
All existing CI jobs still green (web-build, lint-build, unit, integration-cassandra, docker-build, e2e, e2e-full, e2e-ui, ci-tikv, ci-scylla).
Delete `tasks/prd-prod-observability.md` in closing commit per PRD lifecycle (markdown disposable; Ralph snapshot under `scripts/ralph/archive/` becomes canonical).
